response.justification: While Bogdan has genuine geospatial/GIS experience through GISCollective and Green Map, the role's core requirement is production-grade C++ (or Rust) for routing data pipelines — an area where his profile is thin. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project; his primary professional stack for the past decade has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no demonstrable experience with routing tile generation, navigation algorithms, or traffic signal integration, and Rust does not appear at all. The geospatial work he has done is oriented toward data collection and visualization rather than high-throughput routing pipeline processing.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective platform and Green Map web GIS application, including a FOSS4G 2019 conference talk.
Demonstrated experience building high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O and high-performance databases).
Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development across multiple roles.
concerns: C++ experience is sparse and dated — roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project; no modern, production-grade C++ in any subsequent role.
No Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume or skills list.
No experience with routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or traffic/map data integration — the core technical domain of this role.
